Document:
# Network Synchronization (Netcode)
Network synchronization keeps the server and client perfectly in sync. When something changes on the server, it automatically updates on every connected client.
## The Problem We're Solving
### Without Networking - Only one player (not multiplayer) - Changes only exist on your computer - Other players don't see what you do - Total chaos
### With Networking - Multiple players see the same world - When you move, others see you move - When you take damage, health bar updates for everyone - Server is the source of truth
## How It Works: AutoNetworkedField
The simplest way to sync data is with the [mono]AutoNetworkedField[/mono] attribute:
public sealed partial class HealthComponent : Component { [DataField] public float Health { get; set; } = 100f;
[DataField, AutoNetworkedField] // <- This line syncs to client! public float MaxHealth { get; set; } = 100f; }
When [mono]MaxHealth[/mono] changes on the server: 1. Server sets [mono]health.MaxHealth = 150[/mono] 2. AutoNetworkedField detects the change 3. Network message sent to all clients 4. Clients update their copy 5. UI refreshes automatically
[bold]No manual code needed![/bold]
## When to Use AutoNetworkedField
[bold]Use AutoNetworkedField for:[/bold] - Simple properties that change occasionally - Stat values (HP, mana, stats) - Component flags (isVisible, isLocked) - Status information (onFire, frozen) - Any bool, int, float, string that clients need to see
[bold]Don't use AutoNetworkedField for:[/bold] - Huge arrays (network bandwidth issue) - Local-only data (animation state that doesn't need syncing) - Data that changes every frame (physics might be better) - Sensitive info (don't send passwords!)
## Real Example: Stat Synchronization
Here's how character stats sync to the client:
public sealed partial class OE14CharacterStatsComponent : Component { [DataField] public int BaseStrength { get; set; } = 5;
[DataField, AutoNetworkedField] // <- Syncs to client public int EffectiveStrength { get; set; } = 5;
[DataField, AutoNetworkedField] // <- Syncs to client public Dictionary StrengthModifiers { get; set; } = new(); }
When server calculates new modifiers: 1. Server: updates [mono]StrengthModifiers[/mono] 2. Network: Message sent to all clients 3. Client: Receives and updates its copy 4. UI: Shows "STR: 5 -> 7 (+2 Spell)"
Automatic! No manual network code!
## Server Authority
[bold]CRITICAL:[/bold] The server is always right. Clients can predict, but server values are canonical.
Player clicks to attack: | Client predicts attack (shows swinging animation) | Client sends attack request to server | Server validates: "Is this player allowed to attack? Is target in range?" | Server accepts or rejects attack | Server calculates actual damage: 20 | Server updates defender's health: 100 -> 80 | AutoNetworkedField syncs Health to all clients | Clients see correct health value: 80 (not client's predicted 82)
[bold]Why:[/bold] Prevents cheating. Players can't modify their client to take no damage.
## Network Update Frequency
### How Often Do Updates Happen?
Components are networked at a fixed rate (usually 30 updates per second):
Server Frame: 60 ticks/sec Network Update: 30 packets/sec Client Sees: Smooth interpolated position
This balances accuracy and bandwidth.
### Bandwidth Considerations
Only [bold]changed[/bold] data is sent:
Frame 1: Player moves from X=10 to X=12 -> Send "Position changed to 12"
Frame 2: Player moves from X=12 to X=14 -> Send "Position changed to 14"
Frame 3: Player stands still (no change) -> Send nothing! (bandwidth saved)
## Network Prediction and Interpolation
Clients don't just wait for server updates. They predict ahead:
Server: "Player at X=100, velocity=5" | Client predicts: Next frame will be X=105 | Client predicts: Frame after X=110 | Client shows: Player smoothly moving | Server: "Correction: Player at X=108 (slightly different)" | Client: "Adjust interpolation to match server"
Result: Smooth gameplay even with 100ms network delay!
## Event Network Synchronization
Not all data fits in AutoNetworkedField. Sometimes you need events:
// Server-side public override void Update(float frameTime) { // Player took damage var damageEvent = new DamageEvent(target, 10); RaiseLocalEvent(target, damageEvent); }
This automatically syncs to client because it's a networked event type.
## Preventing Network Cheating
Never trust the client. Always validate on server:
// Bad - trusts client public void CastSpell(EntityUid spellId) { _spellSystem.Cast(spellId); // What if client sends invalid spell ID? }
// Good - validates on server public void CastSpell(EntityUid spellId) { var player = GetCurrentPlayer(); if (!player.HasSpell(spellId)) return; // Reject
if (player.Mana < GetManaCost(spellId)) return; // Reject
_spellSystem.Cast(spellId); // Now it's safe }
Server checks: - Does player have this spell? - Does player have enough mana? - Is player allowed to cast (not stunned, silenced)? - Is target in range?
## Network Visibility
Some data should only go to certain clients:
// Only send health to the owner (not other players) [DataField, AutoNetworkedField(Access = ViewAccessLevel.Private)] public int Health { get; set; }
// Send to everyone [DataField, AutoNetworkedField(Access = ViewAccessLevel.Public)] public string Name { get; set; }
// Only send to admins [DataField, AutoNetworkedField(Access = ViewAccessLevel.Admin)] public int DebugValue { get; set; }
Prevents leaking sensitive information.
## Debugging Network Issues
### Problem: Data not syncing 1. Check: Is the field marked with the AutoNetworkedField attribute? 2. Check: Is the component on a networked entity? 3. Check: Is the property public? 4. Fix: Add AutoNetworkedField to the property
### Problem: Clients out of sync 1. Server is crashing/restarting 2. Network packets being dropped 3. Client prediction too aggressive
### Problem: Network bandwidth too high 1. Too many AutoNetworkedFields updating every frame 2. Solution: Only network critical data 3. Use events for one-time notifications
## Best Practices
[bold]DO:[/bold] - Mark networked fields with the AutoNetworkedField attribute - Always validate on server - Only sync data that players need to see - Assume network will be unreliable (handle lag)
[bold]DON'T:[/bold] - Trust client data for important decisions - Send huge arrays over network - Update non-networked fields for gameplay logic - Assume instant network updates
## Example: Player Takes Damage
1. Client sends: "Player clicked attack on enemy" | 2. Server receives, validates, calculates damage (20) | 3. Server updates: health.Health = 80 (was 100) | 4. AutoNetworkedField detects change | 5. Network message: "Health changed to 80" | 6. All clients receive update | 7. Clients update: health.Health = 80 | 8. UI refreshes: Health bar shows 80/100 | 9. Every player in game sees the correct health
All automatic!
## Navigation
- Need to sync a component field? Use the AutoNetworkedField attribute - Want to send one-time messages? Use networked events - Worried about cheating? Always validate on server - Optimize bandwidth? Only sync critical data
## Summary
- [bold]Server is authority[/bold] — server values are always correct - [bold]AutoNetworkedField[/bold] — automatic synchronization - [bold]Client prediction[/bold] — smooth gameplay despite latency - [bold]Event networking[/bold] — one-time messages to clients - [bold]Validation[/bold] — always verify on server
The framework handles most networking for you. Just mark your fields and let it work!
